introSection: Wildcat was a wooden roller coaster located at Hersheypark in Hershey, Pennsylvania, USA. The ride was the park's fourth roller coaster. Opened in 1996, it was the first roller coaster built by manufacturer Great Coasters International (GCI). It closed on July 31, 2022.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The addition of Wildcat was announced in June 1995. It was named after Wildcat, which operated at Hersheypark from 1923 through 1945.

Construction of the new Wildcat began in August 1995. The ride was originally set to open on May 4, 1996, but this was delayed due to a blizzard. Wildcat opened on May 26, 1996. The ride was located at the back left corner of the park while another wooden roller coaster, Lightning Racer is located at the back right corner of the park. Wildcat was built with a curved drop to reduce nausea and featured an on ride camera.

Hersheypark announced on social media on July 8, 2022 that the ride would close on July 31.

In November 2022, it was announced that the ride would be converted into Wildcat's Revenge.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Wildcat was built on a hill, making it look much larger than it really was.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: Due to the extreme roughness of the ride while operating with its original Philadelphia Toboggan Company trains, they were replaced with GCI's Millennium Flyer rolling stock in 2007.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.content: During the Halloween event, "Hersheypark in the Dark", Wildcat was dubbed "Scaredycat".

fullText:
Wildcat was a wooden roller coaster located at Hersheypark in Hershey, Pennsylvania, USA. The ride was the park's fourth roller coaster. Opened in 1996, it was the first roller coaster built by manufacturer Great Coasters International (GCI). It closed on July 31, 2022.

The addition of Wildcat was announced in June 1995. It was named after Wildcat, which operated at Hersheypark from 1923 through 1945.

Construction of the new Wildcat began in August 1995. The ride was originally set to open on May 4, 1996, but this was delayed due to a blizzard. Wildcat opened on May 26, 1996. The ride was located at the back left corner of the park while another wooden roller coaster, Lightning Racer is located at the back right corner of the park. Wildcat was built with a curved drop to reduce nausea and featured an on ride camera.

Hersheypark announced on social media on July 8, 2022 that the ride would close on July 31.

In November 2022, it was announced that the ride would be converted into Wildcat's Revenge.

Wildcat was built on a hill, making it look much larger than it really was.

Due to the extreme roughness of the ride while operating with its original Philadelphia Toboggan Company trains, they were replaced with GCI's Millennium Flyer rolling stock in 2007.

During the Halloween event, "Hersheypark in the Dark", Wildcat was dubbed "Scaredycat".
